name: Browned Flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 1 cup flour in a dry heavy skillet.
2. Stir constantly over very low direct heat, scraping the flour from the sides and bottom of the pan.
3. Alternatively, heat the flour in a very slow oven (200°F to 250°F) in a very heavy pan, shaking the pan periodically to ensure even browning.
4. Do not let it get too dark or become bitter and lose its thickening power altogether.
5. Properly browned flour has only about half the thickening power of all-purpose flour.
6. Store in a tightly covered jar in a cool place.
